item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed reading this article. Even more so, with the added knowledge of the noise planets make when we recorded the noise with an EMFISIS instrument. It has to be some of the eeriest things ever recorded.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm amazed at how well you pulled off this sense of utter dread, and made it seem effortless. Awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very good. I agree with the above comment about 'nothing scary, but I'm scared' - this evokes a sense of dread and unease better than plenty of other articles triple its length with ever-escalating gore and horror tropes. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This obviously has a literal affect on some of us which doesn't surprise me as I'm sure many of us shared similar nightmares or mental states like this which could be a factor of why some of us are here.

But I have to agree with augmentedspartan a bit. While I'm one of those people who likes the thought of irrational, impending doom, I feel like more could have been added to this. Maybe a few addendums and notes by researches/staff frantically arguing about its class status, only for us to witness the arguing growing more and more frantic all-the-while -A and -B are getting closer and closer?

But don't let me tell you how to write. I haven't even made any entrees yet. This is unique to the point where anything else might make it too much like another SCP. Love how the request to update to Euclid seems solely based on fear and people obviously like it so take another +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kudos! You were inspired by the nameless dread of a childhood nightmare, and you've executed this idea beautifully. You had a lot that could go wrong - dream-based SCPs can come across like bad creative writing assignments, and SCPs that cause irrational fear can be cliché - but you nailed it! It makes sense in the illogical way dreams make sense, plus it's just the right length. Hearty +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's nothing scary about this, but I'm scared. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every time they come together, Adam Sandler releases another "film."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this definitely has a nightmare/dream feel to it, which makes sense considering the origins of the concept. I'm legitimately curious as to what might happen when the two objects touch, and as such there is a lot of tale potential here.

+1. A nice, short skip. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to say there wasn't enough story here, but then I caught the dream logic in the final addendum. That sense of knowing something bad will happen, but not knowing what it is or being able to stop it is something I've dreamt myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea, but the execution won't allow me to upvote. Generally, it's well written, but my biggest gripe comes with the last paragraph of the description. I never quite liked when SCPs make people feel uneasy for no reason (aside from real life phenomena like the uncanny valley effect or such), unless it ties with the rest of the anomaly. But the problem lies with the fact that it makes the end feel redundant. Having people feel uneasy over the possibility of the two objects merging doesn't have the same impact if people are already feeling uneasy just being near them. It just feels weird to have a contrived reason to make people scared about the object, when there's already a real reason they should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I wasn't sure these frequencies would really be audible. I know that human hearing is nominally 20-20k, but people vary. Also, I am a bit older than the average reader, and I wanted to test my hearing. So I made an audio file of these two frequencies, one on stereo left and one on stereo right. I couldn't hear it through my headphones at all, so I took it to my stereo with the really good speakers (and I moved them 3 meters apart, naturally). I had to turn it up really loud, but yes I could hear them, and it was deeply unsettling. Just so you know. : )

This is one of those articles that on its own isn't really that interesting, but because nobody has any clue what would happen if the two points met, it becomes scary. Human beings fear the unknown. Nice and short, with solid execution.

On a side note… Holy shit you're on a roll. First two articles, first two successes. Keep it up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Went to fix it, but you and Shaggydredlocks had beaten me to it. Thanks for that and the +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, sweet and ominous. Good blend, good job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Addendum ZZZZ-1

Don't know if this is intentional or not but I just wanted to point it out.

Otherwise, it's short and simple and I enjoyed it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy the simplicity of this, and how it managed to creep me out.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New SCP, based on a recurring nightmare I had as a kid. I don't know what happens when they touch either; I always woke up.
